properties: Calonemorchis clavula has been studied for its potential therapeutic properties, particularly in traditional medicine. Its extracts have been shown to possess antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory activities. Additionally, research suggests that Calonemorchis clavula may have antiviral properties, making it a subject of interest in the development of new treatments for viral infections. More studies are needed to fully understand the therapeutic potential of Calonemorchis clavula.
constituents: Calonemorchis clavula contains flavonoids, particularly kaempferol and quercetin, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant also contains alkaloids, such as berberine, which have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects. In addition, Calonemorchis clavula has been found to contain terpenoids, including oleanolic acid, which may contribute to its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities. The exact composition of active constituents in Calonemorchis clavula may vary depending on the plant's source and preparation.
